channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Philippines accuses China's forces of harassing fisheries vessels in the South China Sea
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Chinese coast guard ships and a Chinese navy helicopter harassed a group of Philippine fisheries vessels conducting a scientific survey in a hotly disputed area of the South China Sea, forcing them to cancel the operation, the Philippine coast guard said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: South Koreans turn funeral wreaths and K-pop light sticks into political protest tools
item.description: SEOUL (AP) — South Koreans are repurposing flower wreaths and K-pop light sticks as political protest tools amid the nation’s deepest political crisis in decades, sparked by President Yoon Suk Yeol’s short-lived martial law declaration in December.
item.dc:creator: JUWON PARK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Auschwitz was liberated 80 years ago. The spotlight is on survivors as their numbers dwindle
item.description: Commemorations are being held Monday to mark the 80th anniversary of the liberation of the Auschwitz death camp by Soviet troops.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Gera, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: House Democrats urge Rubio to lift foreign assistance freeze
item.description: House Democrats urged President Trump’s Secretary of State Marco Rubio to lift the foreign assistance freeze mandated by an executive order from earlier this week, cutting off most U.S-funded foreign development projects around the world.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Israel and Hamas are set to swap more hostages for prisoners in another test of the Gaza ceasefire
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israel and Hamas were expected to swap more hostages for Palestinian prisoners on Saturday, the second such exchange since a ceasefire began in the Gaza Strip last weekend and another test for the deal.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fighting in Sudan's civil war sets ablaze the country's largest oil refinery, satellite photos show
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Fighting around Sudan 's largest oil refinery set the sprawling complex ablaze, satellite data analyzed by The Associated Press on Saturday shows, sending thick, black polluted smoke over the country's capital.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Putin says he's open to meeting with Trump: Russian TV
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin said during an interview that he is open to meeting with President Trump to discuss topics that are of interest to both Moscow and Washington, including the Kremlin’s near three-year invasion of Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Desperate families await return of 4 female soldiers held by Hamas for 15 months
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Hamas announced Friday that it would release four female soldiers held hostage for 15 months in Gaza, as part of an exchange for dozens of Palestinian prisoners laid out in its ceasefire agreement with Israel.
item.dc:creator: SHLOMO MOR and WAAFA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South African president signs controversial land seizure bill, eroding private property rights
item.description: Opponents of a controversial South African land expropriation bill are calling the legislation a threat to private ownership after it was endorsed by the country's president.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A driver rams an anti-government rally in Serbia's capital and injures one protester
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — A woman rammed a car into a crowd of anti-government protesters in Serbia's capital and injured one of them Friday, police said, as a student-led strike shut down businesses and drew tens of thousands of people to demonstrations around the country.
item.dc:creator: JOVANA GEC, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about the escalating conflict in eastern Congo as rebels close in on Goma
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — Fighting has escalated sharply in recent weeks in eastern Congo, where rebels have seized key towns and are closing in on the city of Goma, the government’s last stronghold in the region bordering Rwanda.
item.dc:creator: MONIKA PRONCZUK and MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Colombia president decrees emergency powers to restore order in coca region wracked by rebel combat
item.description: Colombia's President Gustavo Petro has issued a decree giving him emergency powers to restore order in Catatumbo, a coca-growing region wracked by a deadly turf war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump had ‘fiery’ call with Danish prime minister over Greenland: Report
item.description: President Trump reportedly held a “fiery” call with Denmark’s Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen over the president’s insistence that U.S. control of Greenland is necessary for American national security.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Hamas releases names of 4 female hostages to be released next, possibly violates deal
item.description: Hamas says it will release four female IDF soldiers including Karina Ariev, Daniella Gilboa, Naama Levy and Liri Albag in second hostage release in a possible violation of the ceasefire agreement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel’s military will remain in Lebanon as ceasefire deadline nears
item.description: Israel said its forces will remain in southern Lebanon after UN and Lebanese forces moved too slowly under the truce with Hezbollah established in November, which remains unfulfilled.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu suggests Israel might not complete its withdrawal from Lebanon by a ceasefire deadline
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu suggested Friday that Israel might not withdraw all of its forces from Lebanon by a deadline set in its ceasefire with Hezbollah, and Washington appears prepared to push for an extension.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Putin echoes Trump's claim that conflict in Ukraine could have been avoided had he been in office
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin on Friday echoed U.S. President Donald Trump's claim the conflict in Ukraine could have been prevented had he been in the White House in 2022. He also said Moscow is ready for talks with the U.S. on a broad range of issues.
item.dc:creator: VLADIMIR ISACHENKOV, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Tens of thousands protest in Slovakia over pro-Russia policies of populist leader Fico
item.description: BRATISLAVA, Slovakia (AP) — Tens of thousands of people in Slovakia gathered at squares and street across the country on Friday to voice their opposition to the pro-Russia policies of populist Prime Minister Robert Fico.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Governor of Congo's North Kivu province dies of his wounds in fighting with rebels, authorities say
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — The governor of eastern Congo’s North Kivu province has died from injuries sustained during fighting on the front line, a military spokesman said Friday, as M23 rebels closed in on the provincial capital.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN KABUMBA and MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New pandas make their debut at National Zoo
item.description: The National Zoo announced pandas have returned to Washington and made their public debut Friday. “The wait is OVER: the giant pandas have officially made their public debut at the Smithsonian’s National Zoo and Conservation Biology Institute in Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Trump: Zelensky 'shouldn't have allowed' war with Russia to happen
item.description: President Trump suggested Friday that Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky bore some responsibility for the war that began almost three years ago with Russia's invasion. “Zelensky — I will say this, he wants to settle now. He's had enough.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Taiwan Air Force officer killed after being ‘inhaled’ by fighter jet’s engine
item.description: A Taiwan Air Force officer died after being sucked into the engine of an Indigenous Defense Fighter jet at Ching Chuan Kang Air Base, officials say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tabloids apologize to Prince Harry, admit intruding on Princess Diana
item.description: Prince Harry claimed a monumental victory Wednesday as Rupert Murdoch’s U.K. tabloids made an unprecedented apology.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY and JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: At least 76 people killed in a hotel fire at a ski resort in Turkey
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — A fire raged through a 12-story hotel at a popular ski resort in northwestern Turkey early Tuesday during a school holiday, killing at least 76 people — at least two of them when they jumped from the building to escape the flames, officials said.
item.dc:creator: SUZAN FRASER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: At least 80 people killed in northeast Colombia as peace talks fail, official says
item.description: More than 80 people were killed in northeast Colombia over the weekend after the government failed to hold peace talks with the National Liberation Army.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Putin, Xi vow to ‘deepen’ alliance hours after Trump re-enters the White House
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping and Russian President Vladimir Putin held a video call hours after President Donald Trump re-entered the White House and pledged to "deepen" their partnership.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: World leaders react as Trump re-enters the White House
item.description: Leaders across the globe reacted to Donald Trump once again entering the White House, with some nations issuing messages of congratulations while others took issue with his inaugural comments.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 66 dead after fire erupts at popular ski hotel in Turkey
item.description: At least 66 people died and 51 others became injured in a fire that broke out at a Turkish ski resort hotel on Tuesday. Over 200 guests were staying at the hotel when the disaster struck.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump wants to visit China, strengthen relationship: Report
item.description: The president-elect has vowed to impose tariffs and other mechanisms against China upon his return to the White House.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Gaza ceasefire takes hold and the first handover of hostages is underway
item.description: A ceasefire between Israel and Hamas took hold in the Gaza Strip on Sunday, ushering in an initial six-week period of calm and raising hopes for the release of dozens of militant-held hostages and an end to the devastating 15-month war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
